description: The resilient smart cities context and the relationship to urban sustainability; smart city tools, applications and systems; all hazards risk assessment and the evolving cyber-physical threat; operational and strategic urban resilience planning processes in general and for smart city systems.
preAndCorequisites:
• Minimum fourth-year standing in the Civil Engineering program
  • or permission of the department.
supplementalNotes:
• Credit will be granted for only one of CIVE 411, CIVE 511.
  • Students in other programs may request permission to register in the course.
  • May be offered as a joint undergraduate and graduate class.
title: Resilient Smart Cities
